prompt: Gradient descent optimization visualized as a traveler descending a mountain valley toward the lowest point, illustrated in the style of Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock prints from the Edo period (1603–1868). Use traditional ukiyo-e visual grammar: bold black contour lines, flat areas of unshaded color, visible woodblock texture, asymmetrical composition, strong diagonal movement guiding the eye downward, stylized waves and clouds, limited palette of indigo blue, muted green, ochre, and off-white paper tones. The valley represents the loss landscape, contour lines on the mountain slopes show optimization levels, the traveler follows a zig-zag path downward toward the global minimum at the valley floor. Traditional Japanese print layout, minimal perspective, hand-carved woodblock aesthetic, natural paper texture, no modern elements, no photorealistic shading, no digital gradients.
tradition_approach: Used flat color blocks, bold contour lines, limited indigo palette, asymmetrical composition, and woodblock texture to mimic Edo-period ukiyo-e printmaking.
